questions.q: What was the target suitors had to strike at Draupadi's swayamvara?
options: A golden pot atop a flagstaff
A moving figure of a boar
The eye of a fish revolving overhead
An apple set on a spinning wheel
questions.explanation: The suitor had to pierce the eye of a fish revolving high above the arena, a feat designed to defeat almost everyone.

questions.q: Who won Draupadi's hand by striking the target?
questions.explanation: Arjuna, disguised as a young brahmin, strung the bow and pierced the fish's eye when kings had failed.

questions.q: In what disguise did the Pandavas attend the swayamvara?
questions.explanation: Still presumed dead after the lac house fire, the Pandavas came dressed as brahmins and sat among the priests.

questions.q: In which city was Draupadi's swayamvara held?
questions.explanation: The contest took place at Kampilya, the capital of King Drupada's Panchala.

questions.q: What did a suitor have to do before even shooting at the target?
options: String the colossal bow set up for the contest
Recite his lineage for seven generations
Lift a chariot wheel above his head
questions.explanation: Merely stringing the massive bow defeated most of the assembled kings before they could attempt a shot.

questions.q: Which famed warrior was turned away at the swayamvara because of his charioteer lineage, in the most popular retelling?
questions.explanation: In the widely told version Draupadi refused to wed a charioteer's son; the critical edition instead has Karna simply fail at the bow.

questions.q: What did Kunti believe her sons had brought home when she told them to share it among themselves?
questions.explanation: Without looking up, Kunti assumed the brothers had returned with alms and told them to share it, unknowingly speaking of Draupadi.

questions.q: Where were the Pandavas lodging in Kampilya during the swayamvara?
questions.explanation: Living as begging brahmins, the Pandavas and Kunti stayed in the humble house of a potter.

questions.q: Who recognized the disguised Pandavas at the swayamvara despite their brahmin garb?
questions.explanation: Krishna quietly pointed the brothers out to Balarama, recognizing his cousins beneath the disguise.

questions.q: How many husbands did Draupadi ultimately wed as a result of the swayamvara?
questions.explanation: Through Kunti's unwitting command and divine destiny, Draupadi became the shared wife of all five Pandava brothers.

questions.q: In popular retellings, how were suitors required to sight the revolving fish?
options: Through the spokes of a turning wheel
By looking only at its reflection in a vessel below
Over the shoulder while facing away
questions.explanation: Later retellings describe aiming at the fish's reflection in water below, while the older text speaks of shooting through a revolving device.

questions.q: How did the assembled kings react when a seeming brahmin won the princess?
options: They saluted his skill and departed in peace
They demanded the contest be rerun the next day
They petitioned Drupada to divide the prize
They attacked in fury that kshatriyas had been bested
questions.explanation: Enraged that Drupada would give his daughter to a brahmin over royal suitors, the kings rushed at him and the victors.

questions.q: Which king did Bhima battle during the brawl that followed the contest?
questions.explanation: Bhima grappled with the mighty Shalya of Madra and hurled him to the ground, stunning the hostile kings.

questions.q: What did Bhima wield as a weapon in the scuffle after the contest?
questions.explanation: Bhima tore up a tree, stripped its leaves, and stood guard with it like Yama with his staff.

questions.q: Who secretly followed the victorious brahmins to their lodging to discover their identity?
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna trailed the strangers to the potter's house and listened to their talk of weapons and war, then reported to Drupada.

questions.q: Whom did Drupada secretly hope would win the contest he had designed?
questions.explanation: Drupada framed the impossible bow test with Arjuna in mind, wanting Drona's greatest student as his ally and son-in-law.

questions.q: Whose intervention finally convinced Drupada to allow Draupadi's marriage to all five brothers?
questions.explanation: The sage Vyasa arrived and revealed the divine design behind the marriage, overcoming Drupada's objections.

questions.q: In her former life, how many times had Draupadi asked Shiva for a husband, according to Vyasa's account?
questions.explanation: A maiden in a past life, she begged Shiva five times for a husband of every virtue, and he granted five husbands in her next birth.

questions.q: Who calmed the enraged kings by declaring that Draupadi had been fairly won?
questions.explanation: Krishna told the kings the prize had been won righteously according to the announced terms, and the assembly dispersed.

questions.q: What befell mighty kings like Shishupala and Jarasandha at the contest?
options: They struck the fish but missed its eye
They refused to compete before brahmins
They were barred by Drupada at the gate
They failed even to string the great bow
questions.explanation: One after another the great kings strained at the bow and were thrown down or humbled, unable even to bend it.

questions.q: How many arrows was the victor allotted to strike the mark?
questions.explanation: The terms of the contest provided five ornamented arrows with which to pierce the revolving target.

questions.q: What was Drupada's first reaction on learning all five brothers would marry his daughter?
options: He rejoiced at gaining five warrior sons-in-law
He objected that one woman with many husbands defied dharma
He demanded a fresh contest among the five
He asked Kunti to take back her words
questions.explanation: Drupada protested that a man may take many wives but a woman with many husbands was against custom, until Vyasa persuaded him.

questions.q: When Drupada proposed that Arjuna alone wed Draupadi, how did Yudhishthira reply?
options: That Arjuna must first seek Kunti's blessing
That he would gladly renounce his own claim
That all five would marry her, honoring their mother's word and their unity
That Draupadi herself should choose among the brothers
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira held that their mother's command and the brothers' unbroken unity required Draupadi to be the wife of all five.

questions.q: How did the Pandavas, living in Ekachakra, first learn of the coming swayamvara?
options: From a wandering brahmin who described Draupadi's birth and the contest
From a proclamation nailed to the town gate
From Drupada's invitation carried by horsemen
From a dream sent by Indra to Arjuna
questions.explanation: A traveling brahmin told them of the fire-born princess and her swayamvara, and Vyasa soon counseled them to go to Panchala.

questions.q: Which warrior dueled the disguised Arjuna in the melee after the contest, then withdrew praising the brahmin's skill?
questions.explanation: Karna engaged the unknown brahmin in a fierce archery duel and withdrew, marveling at energy he thought no brahmin should possess.

questions.q: Which celestial tale did Vyasa tell Drupada to justify the five-husband marriage?
options: The churning of the great ocean for nectar
The destruction of Daksha's grand sacrifice
The descent of the river Ganga to the earth
The tale of five former Indras destined to share Shri as wife
questions.explanation: Vyasa revealed that five Indras of former ages were to be reborn as the Pandavas, with the goddess Shri born as their common wife.

questions.q: What did Kunti do upon realizing her command to share the alms actually referred to a bride?
options: She begged Yudhishthira to find a way her words would not prove false
She withdrew her words and blessed Arjuna alone
She asked Draupadi to choose one husband herself
She sent word to Drupada apologizing for the error
questions.explanation: Horrified at her slip, Kunti asked Yudhishthira to devise a course in which her word would remain true and no sin would touch Draupadi.

questions.q: In the critical edition of the Mahabharata, why does Karna fail to win Draupadi?
options: He arrives only after the contest has ended
Draupadi's brother bars him from the arena
He cannot string the bow, missing by a hair's breadth
He strikes the fish but not its eye
questions.explanation: The famous rejection by Draupadi belongs to later popular versions; in the critical edition Karna falls short at the bow itself.

questions.q: Which two Yadava leaders quietly visited the Pandavas at the potter's house after the contest?
questions.explanation: Krishna and Balarama came to the potter's house, saluted Kunti and the brothers, and left quietly so as not to expose them.

questions.q: How did Drupada test whether the brahmin victors were truly kshatriyas?
options: He asked them to recite warrior genealogies
He displayed goods of many trades and watched them linger over the weapons of war
He challenged them to ride untamed horses
He served them food forbidden to brahmins
questions.explanation: Drupada laid out articles of farming, trade, and battle, and the brothers went straight to the arms and war gear, betraying their royal blood.
